div1.entryFree:
Opposition, 1) the act of setting against, of offering for combat: the o. of your person in trial, Hml. V, 2, 178.
2) that which is set against, by way of combat or comparison: your whole plot too light for the counterpoise of so great an o. H4A II, 3, 15. alike conversant in general services, and more remarkable in single --s, Cymb. IV, 1, 14 (i. e. when compared as to particular accomplishments. According to the usual interpretation, == single combats).
3) repugnance, resistance: the liberal o. of our spirits, LLL V, 2, 743. the sin of disobedient o. to you and your behests, Rom. IV, 2, 18. why should we in our peevish o. take it to heart, Hml. I, 2, 100. found no o. Cymb. II, 5, 17.
4) combat, encounter: in single o., hand to hand, H4A I, 3, 99. tilting one at other's breast, in o. bloody, Oth. II, 3, 184.
